zucchini-bread-with-oats.name: Zucchini Bread With Oats
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Spray two 8 1/2x4 1/2" loaf pans with nonstick spray and line with parchment paper, leaving a generous overhang on long sides. If using, spread walnuts on a rimmed baking sheet and toast until slightly darkened and fragrant, 8–10 minutes; let cool, then coarsely chop.

Whisk eggs, oil, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and vanilla in a large bowl until smooth. Whisk flour, salt, cinnamon, baking soda, baking powder, and nutmeg in another large bowl just to combine. Make a well in the center of dry ingredients, add egg mixture, and slowly incorporate dry ingredients with a fork (batter will look dry). Fold in zucchini, walnuts, and 1 cup oats. Scrape batter into prepared pans.

Toss raw sugar and remaining 1/4 cup oats in a small bowl. Sprinkle over batter and bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, 70–80 minutes. Transfer pans to a wire rack and let bread cool in pans 30 minutes; turn out bread onto rack and let cool completely.
DO AHEAD: Bread can be baked 4 days ahead. Store tightly wrapped at room temperature.

zucchini-moussaka-lezzet.name: Zucchini Moussaka
instructions: 1. roast a pot with 5 tablespoons of margarine, putting small chopped onions.
2. Then roast with a mixture of 15 minutes until it attracts the water of the mins by adding the mint to the onions.
3. Then cut the tomatoes that are peeled and small chopped, and after 5 minutes, add chopped zucchini to small pieces in one and half fingers.
4. Close the cover of the pot and mix the colors of the pumpkins up to rotating for 5 minutes.
5. Turn on the colors 1 teaspoon of red pepper, half tablespoon salt, 2 cups of water or meat water with fine chopped mint.
6. Close the cover by placing oil paper on top.
7. Cook the zucchini until an hour until softening.
8. Then take the pot from fire and serve by adding the chopped dereot on top.
9. You can find the recipe of eggplant musakka, which is often included in Turkish cuisine.

zucchini-stuffed-with-quinoa-a-very-vers.name: Zucchini Stuffed With Quinoa: A Very Versatile Recipe
zucchini-stuffed-with-quinoa-a-very-vers.original_name: Calabacines rellenos de quinoa: una receta muy versátil

ingredients.item: 1/2 taza de queso feta o queso rallado ((opcional))

instructions: Wash pumpkins and cut them through half. With the help of a spoon, carefully remove his pulp and leave a thick border so that the halves will maintain their shape. Reserve your pulp.

Rinse the quinoa under cold water and cook it into a saucer with the 2 cups of water or vegetable stock. Cooking at medium heat for about 15 minutes or until soft and water has been completely absorbed. Reserve.

In a frying pan, heats olive oil and pits onion and garlic for about 3-4 minutes till they are golden. Add the peppermint, carrot and pulp of minced pumpkins. Cooking everything for about 5 more minutes till the vegetables are tender.

Add the cooked quinoa to salted vegetables and mix well. Incorporates parsley, cumin, peppermint, salt and pepper to suit. If you choose to use Feta cheese, add it at this time as well for a creamy touch.
Place pumpkin halves on a baking plate. Fill them with a mixture of quinoa and vegetables. If you wish, you can cover them with some tomato sauce and grated cheese to gratify.
It purifies the oven to 180 been 3 been (177 been 3) and purifies pumpkins for 25 to 30 minutes, until they are tender and cheese, if you use them, is golden.
